Local Search Progress Visualization¶

In [1]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from pathlib import Path

plt.style.use('seaborn-v0_8-darkgrid')
plt.rcParams['figure.figsize'] = (14, 8)
plt.rcParams['font.size'] = 11

Load the Data¶

In [2]:
results_dir = Path('results')
csv_files = sorted(results_dir.glob('localsearch_*.csv'))
if not csv_files:
    raise FileNotFoundError('No CSV files found in results/. Run local search first.')

latest_csv = csv_files[-1]
print(f'Loading data from: {latest_csv}')

df = pd.read_csv(latest_csv)
required_cols = {'Run', 'Step', 'BitsPerBase', 'GrammarSize', 'NeighborsEvaluated'}
missing_cols = required_cols - set(df.columns)
if missing_cols:
    raise ValueError(f'Missing expected columns: {missing_cols}')

for col in ['Run', 'Step', 'GrammarSize', 'NeighborsEvaluated']:
    df[col] = pd.to_numeric(df[col], errors='coerce')
df['BitsPerBase'] = pd.to_numeric(df['BitsPerBase'], errors='coerce')
df = df.dropna(subset=['Run', 'Step', 'BitsPerBase', 'GrammarSize', 'NeighborsEvaluated']).copy()
df[['Run', 'Step', 'GrammarSize', 'NeighborsEvaluated']] = df[['Run', 'Step', 'GrammarSize', 'NeighborsEvaluated']].astype(int)
df = df.sort_values(['Run', 'Step']).reset_index(drop=True)

# Position within each run after sorting by Step (includes seed row, usually Step=-1).
df['StepIndex'] = df.groupby('Run').cumcount()

run_summary = (
    df.groupby('Run', as_index=False)
      .agg(
          InitialBitsPerBase=('BitsPerBase', 'first'),
          FinalBitsPerBase=('BitsPerBase', 'last'),
          BestBitsPerBase=('BitsPerBase', 'min'),
          InitialGrammarSize=('GrammarSize', 'first'),
          FinalGrammarSize=('GrammarSize', 'last'),
          MinGrammarSize=('GrammarSize', 'min'),
          StepsObserved=('Step', 'max'),
          TotalNeighborsEvaluated=('NeighborsEvaluated', 'sum')
      )
)
run_summary['AbsoluteImprovement'] = run_summary['InitialBitsPerBase'] - run_summary['FinalBitsPerBase']
run_summary['RelativeImprovementPct'] = np.where(
    run_summary['InitialBitsPerBase'] != 0,
    (run_summary['AbsoluteImprovement'] / run_summary['InitialBitsPerBase']) * 100.0,
    np.nan,
)
run_summary['GrammarSizeDelta'] = run_summary['FinalGrammarSize'] - run_summary['InitialGrammarSize']

print(f'Loaded {len(df)} data points across {df["Run"].nunique()} runs')
df.head() # see data format

Loading data from: results/localsearch_20260216_002815.csv
Loaded 4266 data points across 200 runs
Out[2]:
Run Step BitsPerBase GrammarSize NeighborsEvaluated StepIndex
0 1 -1 3.754761 13 0 0
1 1 0 3.726546 13 3 1
2 1 1 3.676596 13 9 2
3 1 2 3.461154 13 3 3
4 1 3 3.389842 12 16 4

Run Summary¶

In [3]:
run_summary.sort_values('FinalBitsPerBase').reset_index(drop=True).round(4) # see summary and dataframe shape

200 rows × 12 columns

Visualization 1: Bits Per Base Progress¶

In [4]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))

for _, run_data in df.groupby('Run'):
    ax.plot(run_data['StepIndex'], run_data['BitsPerBase'], color='tab:blue', alpha=0.18, linewidth=1)

bits_band = (
    df.groupby('StepIndex')['BitsPerBase']
      .agg(Median='median', P25=lambda s: s.quantile(0.25), P75=lambda s: s.quantile(0.75))
      .reset_index()
)
ax.plot(bits_band['StepIndex'], bits_band['Median'], color='tab:blue', linewidth=2.5, label='Median bits/base')
ax.fill_between(bits_band['StepIndex'], bits_band['P25'], bits_band['P75'], color='tab:blue', alpha=0.22, label='IQR (25-75%)')

ax.set_title('Bits Per Base Progress Across Runs', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Step index within run')
ax.set_ylabel('Bits per base')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
  • at first glance, looks like the trend is getting worse again towards the end, but it's only because the good runs are stopping in a local optimum earlier, so we are only left with the bad runs at the end
  • progress slows down, as expected, but not as strongly as first expected

Visualization 2: Grammar Size Evolution¶

In [5]:
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))

for _, run_data in df.groupby('Run'):
    ax.plot(run_data['StepIndex'], run_data['GrammarSize'], color='tab:orange', alpha=0.18, linewidth=1)

size_band = (
    df.groupby('StepIndex')['GrammarSize']
      .agg(Median='median', P25=lambda s: s.quantile(0.25), P75=lambda s: s.quantile(0.75))
      .reset_index()
)
ax.plot(size_band['StepIndex'], size_band['Median'], color='tab:orange', linewidth=2.5, label='Median grammar size')
ax.fill_between(size_band['StepIndex'], size_band['P25'], size_band['P75'], color='tab:orange', alpha=0.22, label='IQR (25-75%)')

ax.set_title('Grammar Size Evolution Across Runs', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Step index within run')
ax.set_ylabel('Grammar size')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
  • grammar size falls, but not monotonically
  • sweet spot seems to be 9, do we have an good theory for why?
  • interestingly, the seed with biggest size (20) doesn't drop 11 steps ?!?

Visualization 3: Relative Improvement Over Normalized Progress (Line-Based)¶

In [6]:
progress_df = df.copy()
progress_df['RunMaxStepIndex'] = progress_df.groupby('Run')['StepIndex'].transform('max')
progress_df['NormalizedProgress'] = np.where(
    progress_df['RunMaxStepIndex'] > 0,
    progress_df['StepIndex'] / progress_df['RunMaxStepIndex'],
    0.0
)

initial_bits = progress_df.groupby('Run')['BitsPerBase'].first().rename('InitialBits')
progress_df = progress_df.merge(initial_bits, on='Run', how='left')
progress_df['RelativeImprovementPct'] = np.where(
    progress_df['InitialBits'] != 0,
    (progress_df['InitialBits'] - progress_df['BitsPerBase']) / progress_df['InitialBits'] * 100.0,
    np.nan
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))
for _, run_data in progress_df.groupby('Run'):
    run_data = run_data.sort_values('NormalizedProgress')
    ax.plot(run_data['NormalizedProgress'], run_data['RelativeImprovementPct'], color='tab:green', alpha=0.15, linewidth=1)

bins = np.linspace(0, 1, 21)
progress_df['ProgressBin'] = pd.cut(progress_df['NormalizedProgress'], bins=bins, include_lowest=True, labels=False)
impr_band = (
    progress_df.groupby('ProgressBin')['RelativeImprovementPct']
      .agg(Median='median', P25=lambda s: s.quantile(0.25), P75=lambda s: s.quantile(0.75))
      .reset_index()
)
impr_band['BinCenter'] = (impr_band['ProgressBin'] + 0.5) / 20.0

ax.plot(impr_band['BinCenter'], impr_band['Median'], color='tab:green', linewidth=2.6, label='Median relative improvement')
ax.fill_between(impr_band['BinCenter'], impr_band['P25'], impr_band['P75'], color='tab:green', alpha=0.22, label='IQR (25-75%)')

ax.set_title('Relative Improvement vs. Normalized Run Progress', fontsize=14, fontweight='bold')
ax.set_xlabel('Normalized progress (0=start, 1=final step)')
ax.set_ylabel('Relative improvement (%)')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
  • relative progress gets more varied the more steps are considered (expected)
  • early progress is more varied than expected, as some runs seem to be stuck for the first 20% of all steps

Visualization 4: Neighbors Evaluated (Line Charts)¶

In [7]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(16, 6))

neighbors_by_run = run_summary.sort_values('Run')
axes[0].bar(neighbors_by_run['Run'], neighbors_by_run['TotalNeighborsEvaluated'], color='tab:red', alpha=0.85)
axes[0].set_title('Total Neighbors Evaluated by Run', fontweight='bold')
axes[0].set_xlabel('Run')
axes[0].set_ylabel('Total neighbors evaluated')
axes[0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

df_copy = df.copy()
df_copy['CumulativeNeighbors'] = df_copy.groupby('Run')['NeighborsEvaluated'].cumsum()
for _, run_data in df_copy.groupby('Run'):
    axes[1].plot(run_data['StepIndex'], run_data['CumulativeNeighbors'], color='tab:red', alpha=0.16, linewidth=1)

iqr_band = (
    df_copy.groupby('StepIndex')['CumulativeNeighbors']
      .agg(Median='median', P25=lambda s: s.quantile(0.25), P75=lambda s: s.quantile(0.75))
      .reset_index()
)
axes[1].plot(iqr_band['StepIndex'], iqr_band['Median'], color='tab:red', linewidth=2.5, label='Median cumulative neighbors')
axes[1].fill_between(iqr_band['StepIndex'], iqr_band['P25'], iqr_band['P75'], color='tab:red', alpha=0.2, label='IQR (25-75%)')
axes[1].set_title('Cumulative Neighbors Over Run Progress', fontweight='bold')
axes[1].set_xlabel('Step index within run')
axes[1].set_ylabel('Total neighbors evaluated')
axes[1].legend()
axes[1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
  • very few runs evaluated less than 130 runs total
  • some take very long with more than 400 runs
  • if a run explores many neighbours early, it has a much higher chance of terminating early
  • number of neighbours per step gets bigger over time, leading to increasing steepness of most lines

Visualization 5: Aggregate Ranked Lines¶

In [8]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

rank_bits = run_summary.sort_values('FinalBitsPerBase').reset_index(drop=True)
axes[0, 0].plot(rank_bits.index + 1, rank_bits['FinalBitsPerBase'], marker='o', linewidth=2, color='tab:blue')
axes[0, 0].set_title('Ranked Final Bits/Base', fontweight='bold')
axes[0, 0].set_xlabel('Rank (best to worst)')
axes[0, 0].set_ylabel('Final bits/base')

rank_impr = run_summary.sort_values('RelativeImprovementPct', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)
axes[0, 1].plot(rank_impr.index + 1, rank_impr['RelativeImprovementPct'], marker='o', linewidth=2, color='tab:green')
axes[0, 1].set_title('Ranked Relative Improvement (%)', fontweight='bold')
axes[0, 1].set_xlabel('Rank (highest to lowest)')
axes[0, 1].set_ylabel('Relative improvement (%)')

rank_neighbors = run_summary.sort_values('TotalNeighborsEvaluated').reset_index(drop=True)
axes[1, 0].plot(rank_neighbors.index + 1, rank_neighbors['TotalNeighborsEvaluated'], marker='o', linewidth=2, color='tab:red')
axes[1, 0].set_title('Ranked Total Neighbors Evaluated', fontweight='bold')
axes[1, 0].set_xlabel('Rank (low to high)')
axes[1, 0].set_ylabel('Total neighbors evaluated')

rank_steps = run_summary.sort_values('StepsObserved').reset_index(drop=True)
axes[1, 1].plot(rank_steps.index + 1, rank_steps['StepsObserved'], marker='o', linewidth=2, color='tab:purple')
axes[1, 1].set_title('Number of Steps', fontweight='bold')
axes[1, 1].set_xlabel('Rank (low to high)')
axes[1, 1].set_ylabel('Max step observed')

for ax in axes.flat:
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Histograms¶

In [9]:
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 10))

axes[0, 0].hist(run_summary['FinalBitsPerBase'].dropna(), bins=20, color='tab:blue', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
axes[0, 0].set_title('Histogram: Final Bits/Base', fontweight='bold')
axes[0, 0].set_xlabel('Final bits/base')
axes[0, 0].set_ylabel('Frequency')

axes[0, 1].hist(run_summary['RelativeImprovementPct'].dropna(), bins=20, color='tab:green', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
axes[0, 1].set_title('Histogram: Relative Improvement (%)', fontweight='bold')
axes[0, 1].set_xlabel('Relative improvement (%)')
axes[0, 1].set_ylabel('Frequency')

axes[1, 0].hist(run_summary['TotalNeighborsEvaluated'].dropna(), bins=20, color='tab:red', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
axes[1, 0].set_title('Histogram: Total Neighbors Evaluated', fontweight='bold')
axes[1, 0].set_xlabel('Total neighbors evaluated')
axes[1, 0].set_ylabel('Frequency')

axes[1, 1].hist(run_summary['StepsObserved'].dropna(), bins=20, color='tab:purple', alpha=0.8, edgecolor='black')
axes[1, 1].set_title('Histogram: Number of Steps', fontweight='bold')
axes[1, 1].set_xlabel('Max step observed')
axes[1, 1].set_ylabel('Frequency')

for ax in axes.flat:
    ax.grid(True, alpha=0.25)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
  • Ranked Final Bits/Base
    • some runs are heavy negative outliers, but there are no strong positive outliers
    • looks a bit like a bimodal distribution (at 2.9 and 3.2), but maybe also just low sample size
  • Relatiev Improvement
    • looks close to normal distribution with mean of 30%
    • not really notable
  • Total Neighbour Evaluated
    • look like a cutoff at around 130 for most cases
    • few high outliers
  • Number of Steps
    • most runs get force-terminated after 25 steps
    • few get stuck very early
    • decent share terminates after 15 steps

Conclusion¶

  • none of the finding seem that out of the ordinary
  • proposal for next todo: get a better understanding of what a "usual" solution looks like / how most runs behave
  • other possible smaller next steps:
    • some runs improve very fast in the first few steps. why?
    • understand behaviour of negative outlier runs
In [ ]:
